chunk_summary: Dental pain contributes to feeding difficulties and faltering growth in Indian practice. Early recognition, prevention, and referral for caries-related pain are essential to address feeding refusal and growth issues effectively.
learning_objectives: Recognize dental pain as a contributor to feeding difficulties and faltering growth in toddlers.
Understand risk factors for early childhood caries, including prolonged bottle feeding and sugary snacks.
Learn strategies for prevention, including fluoridated toothpaste use and limiting sugary foods to mealtimes.
Develop skills for timely referral and management of dental pain to improve feeding and growth outcomes.
key_facts: Prolonged bottle feeding beyond 1 year increases the risk of early childhood caries.
Sugary drinks and packaged snacks often coexist with milk displacement and grazing, exacerbating caries risk.
Twice-daily brushing with fluoridated toothpaste in age-appropriate amounts is recommended for prevention.
Dental pain can disrupt sleep, increase irritability, and compound poor intake in children.
Behavioral feeding advice alone is insufficient when dental pain is present; pain management and referral are critical.
reference_quote: Indian physician-facing dental guidance emphasizes prevention and early referral for high-risk children. Prolonged bottle feeding beyond 1 year, frequent sugary drinks, and packaged snacks increase early childhood caries risk and often coexist with milk displacement and grazing. (IAP Standard Treatment Guidelines 2022)
document_key_topics: Impact of oral health on growth and nutrition
